files_analyzed.file: dist/el-samples-text/Construction/Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
files_analyzed.source_document: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.docx

scope_periods.description: Last three fiscal years ended December 31, 2023, 2024, along with the most recent available financial information (e.g. year-to-date March 31, 2025 or more recent monthly data as available)
scope_periods.trailing_period: YTD March 31, 2025 or more recent monthly data as available
scope_periods.source: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json

sections.heading: Financial due diligence
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: The deliverable will be in the form of a report and Excel data book.

Unless otherwise noted, our work will concentrate on financial information for the last three fiscal years ended December 31, 2023, 2024, along with the most recent available financial information (e.g. year-to-date March 31, 2025 or more recent monthly data as available).

sections.heading: Quality of earnings
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Analyze historical earnings before interest, taxes, depreciation, and amortization (“EBITDA”) at segment levels including understanding the impact of potential non-recurring or non-operation items such as:
Obtain and comment on the Seller’s proposed adjustments analysis;
Working with Client, understand potential impact with potential change in incentive compensation, dis-synergies, or synergies (if applicable);
Understand the impact of the Company’s JV business in the dredging sector;
The impact of significant accounting policies, provisions, management estimates or adjusting entries on the reported results;
Other large, unusual or non-recurring events or transactions that may have distorted results;
Non-cash items such as the impact of provisions, management estimates, or adjusting entries on the reported results; and
Other potential items discovered in the due diligence process.

sections.heading: Sales and margin trends
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: To the extent available, obtain and read an analysis of Target’s revenues and margins and inquire about the following:
Analyze revenue, margin, pricing and average project length on a project basis by the following (where available):
Revenue stream (civil work, special projects (buildings), services and equipment rental);
Customer
Geography
Industry
Understand Target’s job costing methodology (components included in margin, and periodic true ups)

Understand Target’s revenue recognition and billing process (e.g. percentage of completion, periodic and contract completion true-ups (and its variability), loss provision process, policies in place to control accounting recognition and policy escalations, and lifecycle), with specific focus on Construction Management contracts, and its corresponding timing impact to EBITDA;
Understanding the profile (and exposure) of major project/contracts (i.e. Indigenous Partnership with C&C Horizon Contracting Ltd.);
Obtain and analyze the backlog and active backlog as at latest available date (backlog margin on a project basis, and analysis of historical backlog conversion)

sections.heading: Cost of sales and operating costs
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s expenses and inquire about:
Costing methodology;
Cost of sale trends, impact of material change in vendors (if any);
Employee compensation and related costs by function;
Selling, general, and administrative expenses;
Repairs and maintenance expense;
Unusual and extraordinary items (if any).

sections.heading: Working Capital
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of working capital and inquire about the following on a combined basis:
Understand the composition of individual working capital accounts to determine sufficient working capital needs of the Company (including bonding requirements);
Monthly trending and fluctuations in working capital and impact on reported metrics (e.g. days sales/payables/inventory outstanding, net working capital as a percentage of revenues, etc.);

Large, unusual, or non-operating items and current or upcoming initiatives that may affect normalized working capital trends, such as exited business, settlements, capital projects, understanding non-trade balances, etc.
Gain an understanding of the nature of the Company’s unearned revenue.

sections.heading: Accounts receivable
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of the Target’s accounts receivable and inquire and comment on:
Billed/unbilled aging analysis, turnover and days sales outstanding;
Credit terms;
Trade and non-trade balances;
Allowance for uncollectible accounts and write-offs.

sections.heading: Prepaid materials and other assets
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Prepare a summary of other assets (including prepaid materials, prepaid assets) and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances

sections.heading: Inventory
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Prepare a summary of inventory and obtain an understanding of the Company’s inventory costing methodology, tracking of inventory, work-in-progress and obsolescence reserve.

sections.heading: Accounts payable and accrued liabilities
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain and read an analysis of Target’s accounts payable and accrued liabilities and inquire about:
Terms of trade with major suppliers, significant vendor allowances, rebates and/or purchase commitments;
Trade payables aging analysis and days payable outstanding analysis;
Accrued liabilities;
Billings in excess of cost profile; and
Other current and non-current liabilities.

sections.heading: Fixed asset equipment and vehicles
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain an understanding of the Company’s current equipment, including:
Mix of owned versus leased equipment/ vehicles;
Average age of equipment/ vehicles;
For leased fleet, understand the annual payment requirements;
For owned fleet, obtain details of historical capital expenditures and comment on planned, committed, and deferred capital expenditures.

sections.heading: Net debt and other assets/liabilities
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Prepare a summary of debt and debt-like items (e.g. notes payable, letters of credit, capital vendor payables, deferred revenue, pension deficit, warranty matters, exposure from onerous contracts, contingent matters, etc.), other assets, and liabilities and comment on unusual items, significant fluctuations and significant balances.
Summary of lease right-of-use assets and liabilities.

sections.heading: Related parties
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Inquire about Target’s transactions (e.g. income statement and balance sheet impacts) with related parties including subsidiaries, holding companies, joint ventures, variable interest entities, and other entities under common ownership.

sections.heading: Commitments and contingencies
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Obtain and comment on any contingent claims or liabilities including:
Contractual obligations, including leases;
Pending or threatened litigation or investigation by regulatory or other authorities; and
Other off-balance sheet transactions (including contingencies and guarantees).

sections.heading: Accounting overview
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Understand and summarize the financial statement preparation process, including:

Finance function, financial reporting framework, and management reporting relationships (including process, timing, structure of financial reporting, and interaction with the information/ accounting systems);
Integration of recent acquisitions and status of any ongoing integration processes;
Significant accounts estimates and any recent or contemplated changes in accounting principles, procedures, or estimates.
If available, understand the reviewed statements unrecorded differences, management letter points, and other significant findings encountered during the annual review.

sections.heading: Accounting comparison (IFRS vs ASPE)
appears_in: Project Great White - Draft Scope of Work.json
bullets: Target’s accounting policies and the key differences with IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Bird Construction

Analyze the accounting policies as disclosed in the audited financial statements and perform a high-level gap assessment to identify differences between the Target’s accounting policies and practices and IFRS Accounting Standards as applied by Bird Construction Inc.

(Following the high-level gap assessment, if a more detailed gap assessment is required, we will work with Client to identify topics for further analysis and the estimate fees for the incremental analysis.)

workstreams_found.notes: Separate workstream focused on quantifying synergies from the transaction; includes target operating model, functional benchmarking, and bottom-up cost modelling
Separate workstream covering corporation income tax, indirect tax, employment taxes and CEWS for the Target
Optional workstream for tax modeling and structuring assistance, subject to additional fees

notes: This SOW (Project Great White) is for a construction/civil engineering company (Fraser River Pile & Dredge) being acquired by Bird Construction Inc.
The document does not split into phases (e.g., pre-bid vs post-bid). All FDD scope is presented as a single phase.
The SOW includes an IFRS vs ASPE accounting comparison section, which is specific to Canadian targets reporting under ASPE being acquired by an IFRS reporter.

Industry-specific terms like 'retention holdbacks', 'change orders', and 'subcontractor arrangements' are typical construction FDD terms noted by the story prompt but do not appear verbatim in this particular document. They are included in industry_specific_terms for completeness as they are standard construction FDD scope items.
The 'Billings in excess of cost profile' bullet under Accounts payable is a construction-specific item related to percentage-of-completion accounting.
Revenue analysis focuses on project-based metrics: revenue stream (civil work, special projects, services, equipment rental), customer, geography, and industry — all analyzed on a per-project basis.
